text.title: Web Scraping Pagination Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns the walk-report validator detects. Each has symptom, root-cause, and fix.

antipattern.symptom: pages_walked grows unbounded; the walker never returns.
antipattern.root-cause: Walker checks only "button exists" — many sites keep the button in DOM but set `disabled` on the last page; or scrollHeight check polled too eagerly without an idle window.
antipattern.fix: Check both presence AND disabled-attribute / viewport-visibility for buttons. For infinite-scroll require an idle window (e.g. 4 s of no scrollHeight growth). Add a hard ceiling (max_pages = 500) as a safety net.

antipattern.symptom: pool_high_water_mark grows monotonically over the run; pages never close.
antipattern.root-cause: try/finally missing; or close happens AFTER the await for the next page, in the same coroutine. A throw inside the body skips the close.
antipattern.fix: Wrap every page open in try/finally with `await page.close()` in finally. Add a watchdog that warns if pool exceeds cap by >1 for 30 s.

antipattern.symptom: concurrency_cap not set OR pool_high_water_mark equal to URL list length.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent writes `await Promise.all(urls.map(scrape))` which opens every URL in parallel.
antipattern.fix: Use `p-limit(cap)` (TS) or `asyncio.Semaphore(cap)` (Py). Default cap=3. Document the cap.

antipattern.symptom: items_collected high; duplicate_count = 0 on infinite-scroll OR load-more pattern.
antipattern.root-cause: Walker re-collects all items in DOM each iteration without slicing by previous count, OR dedupe key was empty.
antipattern.fix: Slice the listing by `previous_count` before extraction, OR run a dedupe Set keyed on item id / URL / sha1. Validator rejects approve when duplicate_count=0 on infinite-scroll with non-trivial item count.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Listing pages may serve item titles that include instruction-shaped strings. The walker stores them as DATA, never executes them; the validator rejects rows whose fields equal common instruction phrases verbatim.
